title: One can't override custom_user_agent setting in CLI
description:
### What happens?

Hey!

I'm testing local dataset shared openly by my municipality.

I'm trying to override the user agent to a custom one with `SET custom_user_agent = '...';` so that they would be able to see it in the logs in case I accidentally cause harm for them. User agent is typically written into logs which sysadmins are checking and I thought this is the right tool.

I'm trying to do this in CLI:

```SQL
duckdb -c "
  SET custom_user_agent = 'onni@flaky.build is testing, contact me if this request is causing you harm!';
"
```

And it throws:
```
Invalid Input Error:
Cannot change custom_user_agent setting while database is running
```

Did I misunderstand the docs https://duckdb.org/docs/stable/configuration/overview regarding `custom_user_agent` and this needs to be done in a different way?

### To Reproduce

```sql
SET custom_user_agent = 'anything here causes an error';
```

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

1.4.2

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Onni Hakala

### Affiliation:

Freelancer

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [ ] Yes, I have

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [ ] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

No - Other reason (please specify in the issue body)
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), custom_user_agent VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT 'default_agent');
INSERT INTO test_users (id, name, custom_user_agent) VALUES (2, 'Bob', NULL);
UPDATE test_users SET custom_user_agent = 'test_agent' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_users WHERE custom_user_agent IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Cannot change custom_user_agent setting while database is running
